trivia_bullets.text: Secret Plans is a two-color (G/U) uncommon enchantment from Khans of Tarkir (September 2014), supporting morph — the set's cross-faction deception mechanic. KTK design framed morph as the 'sense of mystery and intrigue' defining a warlord-centric setting.
sources: Wizards of the Coast: Khans of Tarkir Design Handoff Part 2

trivia_bullets.text: Secret Plans has 4 unique printings: KTK (2014), Commander 2019 'Faceless Menace' morph precon, Time Spiral Remastered, and a 2023 Arena-only KTK variant. Inclusion in the Commander 2019 morph precon confirms it as a face-down-strategy staple.

Flavor text: 'Rakshasa trade in secrets, amassing wealth from their careful revelation.' The rakshasa are Tarkir's demonic tiger-headed beings who operate as power brokers for the Sultai Brood, sharing secrets in exchange for compensation.
sources: CBR.com: MTG Tarkir's Sultai Brood Clan, Explained

trivia_bullets.text: A recognized staple in morph/disguise Commander builds under Kadena, Slinking Sorcerer — functions as a draw engine alongside Trail of Mystery, turning every face-up trigger into a card. The +0/+1 buff gives face-down 2/2 morphs 2/3 defensive stats.
sources: EDHREC: Kadena, Slinking Sorcerer (via WebSearch snippet)
Moxfield: Kadena Secret Plans deck (via WebSearch snippet)

related_cards.name: Trail of Mystery
related_cards.note: Companion morph-matters enchantment; frequently paired as the two-card morph engine

related_cards.name: Kadena, Slinking Sorcerer
related_cards.note: Face commander of Faceless Menace precon that reprinted Secret Plans; Sultai morph commander

related_cards.name: Ixidor, Reality Sculptor
